description:
Many of the collections in IDC have limited annotations due to the expense and effort required to create these manually. The increased capabilities of AI analysis of radiology images provides an opportunity to augment existing IDC collections with new annotation data. To further this goal, we trained several nnU-Net based models for a variety of radiology segmentation tasks from public datasets and used them to generate segmentations for IDC collections.

To validate the models performance, roughly 10% of the predictions were manually reviewed and corrected by both a board certified radiologist and a medical student (non-expert). Additionally, this non-expert looked at all the ai predictions and rated them on a 5 point Likert scale .

This record provides AI segmentations, manually corrected segmentations, and manual scores for the inspected IDC Collection images. Please see the BAMF-AIMI-Annotations wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This dataset contains image annotations derived from The Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Collection (CPTAC-CCRCC) . This dataset was generated as part of a National Cancer Institute project to augment images from The Cancer Imaging Archive with annotations that will improve their value for cancer researchers and artificial intelligence experts.

Please see the CPTAC-CCRCC-Tumor-Annotations wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This dataset contains image annotations derived from The Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma Collection (CPTAC-PDA) . This dataset was generated as part of a National Cancer Institute project to augment images from The Cancer Imaging Archive with annotations that will improve their value for cancer researchers and artificial intelligence experts.

Please see the CPTAC-PDA-Tumor-Annotations wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This dataset contains image annotations derived from The Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium Uterine Corpus Endometrial Carcinoma Collection (CPTAC-UCEC) . This dataset was generated as part of a National Cancer Institute project to augment images from The Cancer Imaging Archive with annotations that will improve their value for cancer researchers and artificial intelligence experts.

Please see the CPTAC-UCEC-Tumor-Annotations wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This dataset contains standardized DICOM representation of the volumetric nodule annotations and characterizations collected by the LIDC/IDRI initiative.

Please see the DICOM-LIDC-IDRI-Nodules wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains clinical metadata corresponding to several breast imaging collections harmonized into DICOM SR representation, generated by the Data Integration & Imaging Informatics (DI-Cubed) project.

Please see the DICOM-SR-Breast-Clinical wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains expert annotations of bounding boxes identifying locations of the tumor in the Lung-PET-CT-Dx collection in the individual slices of the images. The annotations are accompanied by the assignment of tumor type.

Please see the Lung-PET-CT-Dx-Annotations information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This collection contains expert volumetric segmentations of the suspicious lesions in the NLST collection CT images along with the radiomics features extracted from the segmented regions.

Please see the NLSTSeg information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This dataset contains expert annotations of the suspicious lesions identified in the CT images of the NLST collection. The annotations are stored as planar bounding boxes.

Please see the NLST-Sybil information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

Public imaging datasets are critical for the development and evaluation of automated tools in cancer imaging. Unfortunately, many of the available datasets do not provide annotations of tumors or  organs-at-risk, crucial for the assessment of these tools. This is due to the fact that annotation of medical images is time consuming and requires domain expertise. It has been demonstrated that artificial intelligence (AI) based annotation tools can achieve acceptable performance and thus can be used to automate the annotation of large datasets. As part of the effort to enrich the public data available within NCI Imaging Data Commons (IDC) (https://imaging.datacommons.cancer.gov/) [1], we introduce this dataset that consists of such AI-generated annotations for two publicly available medical imaging collections of Computed Tomography (CT) images of the chest.

Please see the nnU-Net-BPR-annotations description page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains automatic nucleus segmentation data of 5,185 whole slide tissue images of 14 cancer types earlier published in Pan-Cancer-Nuclei-Seg, and stored both in DICOM Microscopy Bulk Simple Annotation format and as DICOM Segmentation objects. The annotations correspond to digital pathology images from the TCGA-BLCA, TCGA-BRCA, TCGA-CESC, TCGA-COAD, TCGA-GBM, TCGA-LUAD, TCGA-LUSC, TCGA-PAAD, TCGA-PRAD, TCGA-READ, TCGA-SKCM, TCGA-STAD, TCGA-UCEC, TCGA-UVM collections available from the NCI Imaging Data Commons.

Please see the Pan-Cancer-Nuclei-Seg-DICOM information page to learn more about the data and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This dataset contributes DICOM-converted annotations of the pancreas to the publicly available National Cancer Institute Imaging Data Commons Pancreas-CT collection. The Pancreas-Ct collection was initially released by The Cancer Imaging Archive (TCIA). While the images in this collection are stored in the standard DICOM format, the collection is also accompanied by 80 manual segmentations of the pancreas in the NIFTI format. Converting these NIFTI manual annotations to DICOM allows users to visualize the CT images and corresponding pancreas annotations in public DICOM-based viewers. The DICOM segmentation format includes additional metadata describing the contained segmented structure using standard SNOMED-CT terminology, and allowing users to easily map them to the reference DICOM CT images. The converted DICOM objects can be stored in any DICOM server that supports those objects (including Google Healthcare DICOM stores), and the DICOM Segmentations can be visualized using off-the-shelf tools, such as OHIF Viewer.

Please see the Pancreas-CT-SEG information page to learn more about the data and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This dataset contributes DICOM-converted annotations to the publicly available National Cancer Institute Imaging Data Commons Prostate-MRI-US-Biopsy collection. Originally released by TCIA, the collection was accompanied by 1017 semi-automatic segmentations of the prostate and 1317 manual segmentations of target lesions in the STL format. Although STL is a common and practical format for 3D printing, it is not interoperable with many visualization and analysis tools commonly used in medical imaging research and does not provide any standard means to communicate metadata, among other limitations. To alleviate those deficiencies, we created DICOM Encapsulated 3D Manufacturing Model objects (M3D modality) that includes the original STL content enriched with the DICOM metadata. Furthermore, we created an alternative encoding of the surface segmentations by rasterizing them and saving the result as a DICOM Segmentation object (SEG modality). As a result, the contributed DICOM objects can be stored in any DICOM server that supports those objects (including Google Healthcare DICOM stores), and the DICOM Segmentations can be visualized using off-the-shelf tools, such as OHIF Viewer.

Please see the Prostate-MRI-US-Biopsy-DICOM-annotations information page to learn more about the data and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This collection contains 66 high resolution automatic CNN-based segmentations of the prostate gland for randomly chosen T2-weighted volumes of the SPIE-AAPM-NCI PROSTATEx Challenges (PROSTATEx).

Please see the PROSTATEx-Seg-HiRes wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains prostate’s CNN-based automatic zonal segmentation for 98 cases randomly selected from the SPIE-AAPM-NCI PROSTATEx Challenges (PROSTATEx). The four-class segmentation encompasses the peripheral zone, transition zone, fibromuscular stroma and the distal prostatic urethra.

Please see the PROSTATEx-Seg-Zones wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains point annotations (targets) of the findings identified in the MRI images of the ProstateX collection. The annotations include the point location of the target, assignment of the prostate region where the target is located, determination of whether the finding is clinically significant or not, and the assignment of the Gleason Grade Group (GGG) for biopsied tissue sampled at the clinically significant targets.

Please see the ProstateX-Targets information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

The purpose of this analysis is to determine the variability of lesion size measurements in computed tomography data sets of patients imaged under a “no change” (“coffee break”) condition and to determine the impact of two reading paradigms on measurement variability.

Please see the QIBA-VolCT-1B wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains lung nodule segmentations obtained using segmentation tools that participated in the QIN challenge “moist run” initiative.

Please see the QIN-LungCT-Seg wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

This collection contains manual and automatic segmentations of the gross tumor volume for the RIDER Lung CT collection.

Please see the RIDER-LungCT-Seg wiki page to learn more about the images and to obtain any supporting metadata for this collection.

The RMS-Mutation-Prediction-Expert-Annotations collections contains expert-generated annotations of the regions of interest identified in the pathology slides from the RMS-Mutation-Prediction collection. Annotations were generated manually, using the ImageScope tool, to delineate regions of ARMS (alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma), ERMS (embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma), stroma, and necrosis and stored as planar contours, consequently converted into Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) Structured Report (SR) representation.

Please see the RMS-Mutation-Prediction-Expert-Annotations information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This collection contains algorithmically-derived spatial aggressiveness maps for a subset of microscopy images from the TCGA collections. The data was generated using the GBM360 algorithm developed by Olivier Gevaert lab at Stanford University. These spatial aggressiveness maps indicate the local aggressiveness of different tumor regions, with higher aggressiveness contributing to a worse overall survival, based on the analysis performed by the authors of the algorithm.

Please see the TCGA-GBM360 information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This collection contains segmentations of the tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes for a subset of images from various TCGA slide microscopy images. The segmentations were generated automatically using two distinct algorithms developed by the Joel Saltz lab at Stony Brook University, and include both binary and probabilistic segmentations.

Please see the TCGA-SBU-TIL-Maps information page to learn more about the images and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.

This collection contains results of automatic analysis of the CT images using the TotalSegmentator model to generate volumetric segmentations of anatomic organs, with the subsequent application of the pyradiomics library to extract first order and shape features for the regions defined by those segmentations. Initial release of this collection contains these analysis results for the National Lung Screening Trial (NLST) collection.

Please see the TotalSegmentator-CT-Segmentations information page to learn more about the data and any supporting metadata for this collection, and to learn about attribution/citation requirements.
